title: Trisetopsis newtonii
intro: Trisetopsis newtonii, also known as Newton's beard grass, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Trisetopsis newtonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal activities. In traditional medicine, Trisetopsis newtonii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including coughs, colds, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Trisetopsis newtonii is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Africa, specifically in the savannas and grasslands. The species is classified within the subfamily Pooideae, which includes many different types of grasses. Trisetopsis newtonii is closely related to other species in the genus Trisetopsis. Further classification may be possible at the genus and species levels.
